channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: What can we learn from insulin price reductions
item.description: The Fast Company Impact Council is a private membership community of influential leaders, experts, executives, and entrepreneurs who share their insights with our audience. Members pay annual membership dues for access to peer learning and thought leadership opportunities, events and more.
item.dc:creator: Dorothy Gemmell, Fast Company

item.title: Why your IoT devices are the weakest link in security
item.description: The Fast Company Impact Council is a private membership community of influential leaders, experts, executives, and entrepreneurs who share their insights with our audience. Members pay annual membership dues for access to peer learning and thought leadership opportunities, events and more.
item.dc:creator: Svetlin Todorov, Fast Company

item.title: Saudi Arabia's Vision 2030: A Technology-Driven Transformation
item.description: Saudi Arabia's Vision 2030 emphasizes a technology-driven transformation to achieve its social, economic, and environmental goals, focusing on adopting and producing advanced innovations.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: EU pulls back – for the moment – on privacy and genAI liability compliance regulations
item.description: When the EU on Tuesday said it was not, at this time, moving ahead with critical legislation involving privacy and genAI liability issues, it honestly reported that members couldn’t agree. But the reasons why they couldn’t agree get much more complicated.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Go 1.24 arrives with generic type aliases, boosted WebAssembly support
item.description: Go 1.24, an update to Google‘s popular open source programming language, is now generally available as a production release, with full backing for generic type aliases, performance improvements, and improved WebAssembly support. The release was unveiled February 11, and can be downloaded from Go.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Cooling an RTX 5090 GPU using a 12,000 BTU HVAC? I am not sure that is a good idea at all
item.description: Extreme cooling sounds great, but condensation, energy costs, and complex modifications make this setup far from ideal.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: ChatGPT’s latest feature is a transformative leap for web research
item.description: I’m not sure who first compared ChatGPT to Cliff Clavin, the garrulous mailman/barfly from TV’s Cheers. It’s so an apt comparison that many people probably came up with it independently. Cliff did know a lot of stuff—after all, he was a Jeopardy almost-winner.
item.dc:creator: Harry McCracken, Fast Company

item.title: Apple TV is finally available on Android devices – just in time to stream MLS and Severance season 2
item.description: You can now download the Apple TV app on Android devices via the Google Play Store to stream Apple TV+ and MLS Season Pass.
item.dc:creator: amelia.schwanke@futurenet.com (Amelia Schwanke), TechRadar

item.title: Google Calendar users no longer see default entries for events like Pride, Black History Month
item.description: Google’s online calendar has removed default references for a handful of holidays and cultural events — with users noticing that mentions of Pride and Black History Month, as well as other observances, no longer appear in their desktop and mobile applications.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Virginia bans DeepSeek from state devices, networks
item.description: Virginia Gov. Glenn Youngkin (R) barred state employees Tuesday from using artificial intelligence (AI) models developed by Chinese startup DeepSeek on state-issued devices and state-run networks.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Lauren Sanchez alludes to ‘turbulence’ in life after ‘obscene’ Trump inauguration display
item.description: The fiancee of billionaire Jeff Bezos alluded to a rough start of 2025, several weeks after Megyn Kelly called her a 'hooker' for wearing a lacy white bustier top beneath a jacket to Trump's swearing-in.
item.dc:creator: Martha Ross, The Mercury News

item.title: How Elon Musk $97.4 billion bid complicates matters for OpenAI
item.description: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman has dismissed a $97.4 billion takeover bid led by rival Elon Musk, but the unsolicited offer could complicate Altman’s push to transform the maker of ChatGPT into a for-profit company.
item.dc:creator: Matt O'Brien, Thalia Beaty And Kelvin Chan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: An AI agent could help you buy your next car
item.description: Capital One has launched an AI agent designed to help customers with one of the more difficult and confusing purchase decisions: buying a car.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Esports Olympics to debut in Saudi Arabia later than expected in 2027
item.description: LAUSANNE, Switzerland (AP) — The first Olympic Esports Games will be hosted in Riyadh in 2027, two years later than expected when a 12-year deal with Saudi Arabia was signed last year.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Ukraine's rare earth elements could help keep US military aid flowing
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine has offered to strike a deal with U.S. President Donald Trump for continued American military aid in exchange for developing Ukraine’s mineral industry, which could provide a valuable source of the rare earth elements that are essential for many kinds of technology.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Top translation apps for travelers
item.description: Travel abroad worry-free with these top translation apps for travelers. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson highlights some favorites.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sam Altman says 'no thank you' to Musk-led group's bid for control of OpenAI
item.description: OpenAI CEO and co-founder Sam Altman appeared to shut down a reported offer from an investor group led by Elon Musk to buy the nonprofit that controls OpenAI, marking the latest back-and-forth between the two tech leaders.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: News influencers favored Trump over Harris during campaign, study finds
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Americans who followed news influencers during the presidential campaign were more likely to hear positive reports about Donald Trump than they were about Kamala Harris, a study has revealed.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: How AI could strengthen citizens’ confidence in government
item.description: "There are some very prominent ones that we see every day, things like at the TSA with facial recognition," said Valerie Wirtschafter. The post How AI could strengthen citizens’ confidence in government first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Microsoft Teams is becoming a prime target for sophisticated scammers
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says hackers are using Microsoft Teams for phishing, vishing and quishing via social engineering.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Huge healthcare data breach exposes over 1 million Americans' sensitive information
item.description: Community Health Center, a Connecticut-based healthcare provider, has disclosed a recent data breach affecting over 1 million people in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: LG recalls 500K electric ovens after fires, pet deaths
item.description: According to the recall, the front-mounted knobs on the recalled ranges can be accidentally activated by humans or pets.
item.dc:creator: Brady Doran, NewsNation

item.title: Nebraska government system shutdown disrupts the Legislature, DMV and other offices
item.description: OMAHA, Neb. (AP) — A systemwide outage of state goverment computers shut down everything from the work of the Legislature to driver’s licensing offices for several hours Thursday.
item.dc:creator: MARGERY A. BECK, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: One Tech Tip: How to block your phone from tracking your location
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Smartphones are useful tools for everyday life, but they're privy to nearly everything about you, including all the places you've been — if you let them.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, NewsNation
